verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (Hsp60) is a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin with canonical inner-compartment localization. However, documented evidence supports surface display in specific contexts: (1) cancer cells exhibit ecto-Hsp60 on the outer leaflet as part of the immunogenic chaperone ecto-pool — a well-characterized phenomenon in tumor biology; (2) cell-death pathways (apoptosis, necroptosis, immunogenic cell death) externalize mitochondrial proteins including Hsp60 to the PM outer face; (3) immune activation in dendritic cells and other antigen-presenting cells drives regulated translocation of Hsp60 to the surface. The baseline mitochondrial matrix residency is clear, but non-baseline ecto-pools in cancer, cell death, and immune-activation contexts qualify as contextual surface accessibility.
